channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Palace seek £55m-plus for Lacroix as talks with Chelsea begin
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Chelsea are in talks with Crystal Palace over the signing of defender Maxence Lacroix.

Olufemi Ajadi Oguntoyinbo, on the occasion of his birthday, expressing confidence that his politi

item.title: Lumentum’s stock just got a lot more interesting — and Barclays has turned bullish
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: A Barclays analyst now recommends Lumentum’s stock after a period of substantial underperformance relative to the broader chip sector.

item.title: “It Will Hit You Hard” –Federer Discloses Sampras’ Advice Ahead Hall Of Fame Induction
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Pete Sampras has advised Roger Federer that his upcoming International Tennis Hall of Fame induction would be a moment like no other that would hit him hard and hit home strong. Ahead of his induction, Federer shared that he asked Sampras what the experience was like, and received the advice that lo

item.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

item.description: The vice-presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Senator Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, has defended himself against allegations of extremism, renewed his opposition to a Muslim-Muslim presidential ticket, and expressed confidence that the NDC ticket of Peter Obi and himself will win

item.title: Nigeria, UK Deepen Security Partnership, Step Up Counter-Terrorism, Cybersecurity Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria and the United Kingdom have agreed to deepen their security and defence partnership, committing to stronger collaboration in counter-terrorism, cybersecurity, maritime security, intelligence sharing, and the fight against fraud and illicit finance. The commitment was contained in a communiqu

item.title: Peter Obi will defeat Tinubu in 2027 – Kwankwaso
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Vice presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Rabiu Kwankwaso, has expressed confidence that the party’s presidential candidate, Peter Obi, will defeat President Bola Tinubu in the 2027 general election. Kwankwaso said the NDC enjoys growing support across different part

item.title: Veritas varsity targets skills revolution with N1.3bn hub
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Veritas University, Abuja, has launched an ambitious drive to redefine graduate employability with the commencement of a N1.3 billion Innovation Hub and Skills Boot Centre in the university campus, declaring that no student will graduate from the institution without acquiring a practical skill. The

item.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company like SpaceX - a giant gamble, and, in my opinion, terribly overpriced - is fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100

item.title: 800,000 Ekiti Residents Captured In Govt Health Insurance Scheme
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: More than 800,000 residents of Ekiti state have so far benefited from the state government Ulerawa Health Insurance Scheme. The Commissioner for Health and Human Services in the state, Dr Oyebanji Filani, who stated this said it is an indication of the government’s unwavering commitment to the cont

item.title: How I was molested at 12 by 40-year-old woman – Peller [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian Tiktok streaming and content creator, Peller, has narrated how he was molested at the age of 12. Peller made the revelation in a snippet of his latest interview on Honest Bunch podcast. Recounting his childhood experience, the content creator said “let me tell you when I lose my virgi

Colonel Apollonia Anele, Acting Director of Army Public Relations, in a statement, categorically sai

item.title: Wall Street’s AI divide: Inside the bull and bear cases for what comes next for the stock market
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Three years into the AI trade, Wall Street is no closer to agreeing about whether it’s a bubble, how much longer it can keep going or what might ultimately end it.

item.title: Abia Govt Intensifies Response To Flood Alert
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎Abia State government has intensified its response to the flood alert by the federal government in the state even as the rain has become heavier since last week. ‎ ‎The commissioner for Information, Okey Kanu stated this in a statement made available to the media in Umuahia, the capital yesterday.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Democratic Congress (NDC) vice-presidential candidate, Rabiu Kwankwaso, has revealed that the party's presidential candidate, Peter Obi, has formally committed to serving only one term if elected president in the 2027 general election. item.description: The Federal Government has said it will proceed with its planned land swap at the Federal Government College (FGC), Kano, despite opposition. The government said the project is aimed at raising funds to expand the school and improve its facilities. The Minister of Education, Dr Maruf Olatunji Alausa

item.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is one of the basic building blocks of AI interoperability, giving AI models a secure way to access external data sources and services. It’s the plumbing that lets a chatbot reach into your calendar, your database, or your internal tools, instead of engineers b

item.title: The PlayStation replica ornament is an homage to a great, yet fragile console
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound. Did you know that it's technically a multi-part chime? There's the synthy section where "Sony Computer Entertainment" shows onscreen with a white background. You only get to the next screen with the echo-y chimes and the color-filled PlayStatio

item.title: The stage never leaves you: Why Nigeria must stop undervaluing the performing arts
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Opinion
item.description: For too long, performing arts in Nigeria have been viewed through a narrow lens. Dance, theatre and cultural performances are read more The stage never leaves you: Why Nigeria must stop undervaluing the performing arts

item.title: Taraba PDP Governorship Candidate Urges Calm After Court Dismisses Turaki Faction Suit
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) governorship candidate in Taraba State, PDP, has urged members and supporters of the party not to lose hope following the recent Federal High Court judgment on the party’s national leadership. In a statement on Monday, Ikenya said there was no cause for panic

item.title: Yemen’s Houthis declare maritime blockade of Saudi Arabia
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Saudi Arabia and the Houthis traded fire last week for the first time in years, threatening a 2022 truce that has held despite expiring. The post Yemen’s Houthis declare maritime blockade of Saudi Arabia appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Iran condemns US attacks on civilian infrastructure
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iran says it has received mediation proposals from multiple parties and insists diplomatic efforts remain active.

item.title: Reps set to pass State Police bill, transmit to State Assemblies
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Barring any last-minute changes, the House of Representatives’ Special Ad-hoc Committee on Constitution Review will, during Tuesday’s plenary, lay its report on the bill seeking to establish State Police Services. The Special Ad-hoc Committee, chaired by Hon. Benjamin Kalu, met on Monday

item.title: NAFDAC blacklists Onifam Laboratories over regulatory violations
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The agency’s boss noted that further investigation uncovered circumvention of regulatory controls through importation, distribution, marketing or sponsorship of regulated products contrary to approved NAFDAC regulatory processes. The post NAFDAC blacklists Onifam Laboratories over regulatory violati

item.title: Messi: It will take time to heal from final defeat and 'immense' pain
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Gunmen ambush, kill two farmers in Plateau
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Suspected gunmen have ambushed and killed two farmers in Takkas community, Fier Ward of Pankshin Local Government Area of Plateau State, in separate attacks that have heightened fears among residents. The post Gunmen ambush, kill two farmers in Plateau appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: BREAKING: Tinubu appoints Fayose, 25 others to federal agencies, commissions
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has appointed former Ekiti State governor Ayo Fayose and 25 others to key positions across 10 federal government agencies and commissions. The appointments were announced in a statement issued on Monday by the President’s Special Adviser on Information and Strategy, Bayo

item.title: I risked my life for Niger Delta peace – Jonathan
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former President Goodluck Jonathan recounts risking his life by entering a militant camp without security to negotiate peace in the Niger Delta, leading to
Read More: https://punchng.com/i-risked-my-life-for-niger-delta-peace-jonathan/

item.title: Tinubu Appoints Fayose REA Chairman, Names 25 Others To Key Federal Agencies
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the appointment of 26 persons into the boards and management of 10 Federal Government agencies and commissions, with former Ekiti State Governor, Ayo Fayose, emerging as Chairman of the Rural Electrification Agency (REA). The appointments, which take immediate effe
